2605.22763.title: Advancing Mathematics Research with AI-Driven Formal Proof Search
2605.22763.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly excel at mathematical reasoning, but their unreliability limits their utility in mathematics research. A mitigation is using LLMs to generate formal proofs in languages like Lean. We perform the first large-scale evaluation of this method's ability to solve open problems. Our most capable agent autonomously resolved 9 of 353 open Erdős problems at the per-problem cost of a few hundred dollars, proved 44/492 OEIS conjectures, and is being deployed in combinatorics, optimization, graph theory, algebraic geometry, and quantum optics research. A basic agent alternating LLM-based generation with Lean-based verification replicated the Erdős successes but proved costlier on the hardest problems. These findings demonstrate the power of AI-aided formal proof search and shed light on the agent designs that enable it.
2605.22763.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在數學推理方面越來越出色，但它們的不可靠性限制了它們在數學研究中的實用性。一種緩解方法是使用LLMs生成像Lean這樣的語言中的正式證明。我們對這種方法解決未解決問題的能力進行了首次大規模評估。我們最強大的代理自主解決了353個開放的Erdős問題中的9個，每個問題的成本為幾百美元，證明了44/492個OEIS猜想，並正在應用於組合學、優化、圖論、代數幾何和量子光學研究。一個基本的代理交替使用基於LLM的生成和基於Lean的驗證，複製了Erdős的成功，但在最難的問題上成本更高。這些發現展示了AI輔助正式證明搜索的力量，並闡明了使其可行的代理設計。
2605.22763.author: George Tsoukalas et.al.
